---
# Member parameters
title: Dr. Maliheh Nazari-Jahantigh
academic_title: Dr. rer. nat. Maliheh Nazari-Jahantigh
first_name: Maliheh
surname: Nazari-Jahantigh
categories:
  - University Hospital

# There is no limit to the number of memberships a person can have. All memberships must have a type, status, and start year.
# See documentation for more details on the different types of memberships and their status options.
memberships:
  - type: lmu-individual
    status: active
    position: Principal Investigator
    faculty: Institute for Cardiovascular Prevention (IPEK)

# if desired by the member, add a photo to the people/people/images folder and link to it here
photo: "images/maliheh_nazari-jahantigh.webp"
email: mnazari@med.lmu.de
personal: |

# certain keywords for the label are recognized and the output is sorted in this priority: LMU Profile, Professional Profile, Personal Website, and Professional Website. see documentation for details on these labels. other links appear in the order they are listed in the YAML file.
links:
  - label: "LMU Profile"
    url: "https://www.ipek-research.com/7f1f5cebeaadfdb5/7f1f5cebeaadfdb5"

# Social media links - variable number
social_media:
  ORCID: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1094-107X"

# Mission statement paragraphs - variable number
mission_statement: |
  I find Open Science important because transparent and reproducible research strengthens both scientific quality and public trust. In my own work, I already value clear documentation, reproducible workflows, and sharing methods and technical insights whenever possible. I am especially interested in improving accessibility of research tools and promoting interdisciplinary collaboration between applied research, data science, and real-world implementation. I see the most important areas of Open Science in reproducibility, open methodologies, and creating research practices that make scientific knowledge easier to verify, reuse, and build upon.

# Research interests - variable number
research_interests:

# Publications - variable number
publications:


# these must be included always.
template: templates/people-template.html
filters: 
    - templates/people_filter.lua
---

<!-- additional content **can** be included here, and appears after the Mission Statement but before the Research Interests. Any content that Quarto can render can be included here, namely R/Python/Julia code chunks, Markdown formatting, more HTML, etc. Main reasons to use this though: code blocks, and to include files. -->
